title: Open Knowledge Format: Building Both Sides
description: Built a producer/consumer implementation of Google's Open Knowledge Format (OKF v0.1) to understand it hands-on: a Postgres-to-OKF generator with LLM-enriched concept docs, a spec conformance validator, and a retrieval + text-to-SQL consumer with no vector database — just keyword scoring and markdown link traversal.
highlights: Producer + consumer implementation — introspects Postgres, generates a spec-conformant bundle, and reads it back to answer questions
Retrieval mechanism is structurally lexical search — same paraphrase-matching weakness measured in an earlier hybrid-search benchmark
Foreign-key relationships become one-hop markdown link traversal at query time — deterministic and inspectable vs. embedding proximity
Zero vector database or embedding index — full pipeline runs on filesystem markdown + git

title: Lexical vs Semantic Search
description: Compared lexical search (Postgres full-text), vector search (pgvector + OpenAI embeddings), and hybrid retrieval (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) on a real corpus of 10 LangChain study notes. Evaluated 15 hand-crafted queries across exact, semantic, and ambiguous buckets with Precision@5 scoring.
highlights: Lexical hit@5 on semantic queries: 0.00 — complete failure on paraphrased intent
Vector hit@5 on exact queries: 1.00 — challenges the common assumption that vector search struggles on keywords
Hybrid doesn't always beat vector alone — a bad lexical signal dilutes RRF fusion on ambiguous queries
Single Postgres database serving both full-text and vector search — no separate search engine needed
